lightBloom.note: The sweep line should be brightest at the leading edge with aggressive bloom that fades into the 30-degree trail behind it
Targets brighten momentarily as the sweep passes over them, then bloom decays with phosphor persistence
Red conflict alert border should bleed red light into surrounding panels on each flash peak

chromaticAberration.note: Subtle radial aberration on the circular radar scope only — the curved CRT glass causes fringing at edges. No aberration on flat panels

filmGrain.note: 0.02 is present. Add radar-specific noise: within the radar scope circle, overlay Perlin noise at 0.04 opacity that rotates WITH the sweep — radar ground clutter texture

scanLines.note: Spacing 4 at 0.03 is very subtle. The radar scope specifically should have CIRCULAR scan lines (concentric rings at 2px spacing, 0.04 opacity) rather than horizontal — it is a PPI display

phosphorPersistence.note: Decay 0.94 is specified. Add color shift during decay: fresh targets are bright #44ff66, decaying targets shift toward #227733 then #113322 before fading — phosphor color temperature drops as it fades

reflectionGlare.note: The PPI scope has a viewing hood. Add a faint circular rim highlight around the scope edge — glass bezel reflection

vignette.note: Apply additional vignette SPECIFICALLY to the radar scope — a circular vignette, inner radius 0.7, outer 1.0, opacity 0.3. Radar edges are always darker than center

cameraDrift.note: Minimal — ATC scopes are fixed workstations. Just enough to prevent static-image feeling

rackFocus.note: When C9G003 conflict alert triggers, focus subtly shifts to the conflict panel — controller's eyes snap to the alarm

dramaticReveals.note: Specified but needs enhancement: as C9G003 completes approach, the entry should flash green briefly, then slide up and fade with a satisfying 'cleared to land' finality. Remaining entries compact upward with spring physics

breathingAnimations.note: The center of the radar (antenna position) should have a faint steady glow that breathes — the transmitter is always radiating
Active flight strips should have their green border subtly pulse — the strip is 'live'

microInteractions.note: Data tags should have very slight positional jitter — they are auto-tracked labels and the system recalculates position each sweep
Wind barb should flutter slightly — wind is never perfectly steady

transitionMoments.description: Landing sequence climax — C9G003 distance decreases to 0, flight strip flashes green, approach ladder entry slides up, conflict alert resolves and dims, controller logs 'C9G003 CLEARED TO LAND'. Brief moment of calm before next sequence begins

dataDensityGaps.fix: Add minimum radar altitude (MRA) contour lines at key terrain elevations around the airport. Add 'RADAR SERVICE TERMINATED' text at the outer range ring edge
Add SLOT TIME field (HH:MM) and SEQUENCE NUMBER. Also add a scratch-pad area per strip — a small text field for controller notes
The delay-info panel needs a DEPARTURE RATE vs ARRIVAL RATE ratio indicator and PREDICTED DELAY trend (arrow up/down/stable)
Add NEXT SECTOR: C9-WEST, HANDOFF FREQ: 128.750 MHz, and sector entry/exit fixes
Add airport diagram in very low opacity (0.04) centered on the radar scope — runway orientation visible beneath the radar returns

colorGradingNotes.overallLook: Dark green scope with desaturated side panels — grade like the darkened TRACON room in Pushing Tin. Key color is phosphor green. Side panels should be cooler and more blue-gray (#0e1212) to contrast with the warm green scope

keyMoments.shift: Red bleed from the conflict alert panel washes across the right side at 0.03 opacity — the alarm contaminates the visual space
Overall composition brightens 3% and conflict red fades out — tension release

colorGradingNotes.accentUsage: Weather overlay colors (yellow/orange/red) are well chosen. But the strip background (#1a1a22) is too blue for a green-dominant palette. Shift to #1a1c1a for consistency
colorGradingNotes.backgroundAtmosphere: The radar scope needs a subtle radial gradient — center #0e160e, edges #0a100a. The phosphor emits more light at center. Side panels should be flat dark

typographyNotes.fonts: Fira Code is excellent — ligatures give a modern professional feel to the data tags
typographyNotes.monospaceReadout: Fira Code at weight 600 for readouts is clean and wide. Perfect for radar tags where single-character misread could be dangerous
typographyNotes.displayFont: For the UTC clock (24px), consider using the font at weight 700 with slight letter-spacing (1px) for authority
typographyNotes.sizeHierarchy: 9px labels are too small for ATC — real controllers need instant readability. Bump ALL labels to 11px minimum. Flight strip text at 10px should be 11px. Radar tags at 10px are acceptable for the reduced scope

continuousAmbience.sound: Soft rhythmic whoosh every rotation — the sweep has a sound presence, like a washing machine on low
Constant low hum of electronics and air conditioning — the TRACON room is a sealed, climate-controlled cave

eventSounds.sound: SHARP two-tone alarm: D5-A5-D5 rapid repeat at 2Hz — the MSAW/conflict alert is the most terrifying sound in aviation
Squelch click + pilot/controller voice cadence — brief radio crackle
Satisfied double-click — the strip is removed from the bay

silenceMoments.description: Brief pause before C9G003 touchdown — the controller watches the scope in silence. No new comms entries. The room is watching this one land
